behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestrator-to-reviewer module handoff (document fan-out + role-specific pack binding), not model judgment. A unit test of one helper could miss miswired role routing across dispatcher/client modules. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is only about what is sent ("sends ... correct ... instruction pack"), not whether reviewer output is correct.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real dispatcher + reviewer client adapters with in-process test doubles for the three reviewer endpoints. Execute one dispatch, assert each role receives identical document bytes/hash and its own instruction pack, with no cross-role pack swap.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model extraction quality on legal text. A stubbed extractor would always return expected terms and hide real misses/false negatives. Rejected Workflow because behavior covers one reviewer’s single model decision, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the live clause-extractor model on curated real contracts with gold annotations for parties/dates/obligations/penalties; score recall/precision thresholds and fail on missed key terms.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is payload contract correctness across modules (config loader/orchestrator/risk-client). Real model output is not required to catch policy-drop or field-mapping bugs. Rejected Agentic because text is about configuration passing ("is passed correctly"), not assessment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate policy config + orchestration path with in-process risk client spy; invoke assessor call and assert serialized request contains exact policy version, thresholds, and enabled rules expected by the risk assessor contract.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets semantic detection quality (including indirect/euphemistic phrasing), which depends on real model judgment and variability. Mocked responses hide this failure mode. Rejected Workflow because this is one reviewer’s output quality, not chained agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Send policy + contracts containing direct and euphemistic risky clauses to the live risk model; assert required risky spans are flagged with acceptable recall and bounded false positives.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module interface: context propagation into compliance request. In-process wiring test catches dropped/wrong jurisdiction/profile mapping. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is only parameter passing ("are passed correctly"), not compliance decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use real orchestration + compliance request builder with a spy transport; invoke check with multiple jurisdiction/profile combinations and assert outbound payload preserves exact codes, profile IDs, and effective dates.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: This is model/legal reasoning correctness on real requirements, not deterministic plumbing. Stubs would mask real misclassification of compliance status. Rejected Workflow because behavior focuses on one checker’s decision, not multi-agent sequence quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run live compliance model on contracts with expert-labeled pass/fail outcomes across jurisdictions; assert decision accuracy and critical-requirement coverage meet acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is merge logic/representation for conflicting inputs; exact failure can be reproduced by pure function/object test with deterministic reviewer outputs. Rejected Integration because text scopes to merge rule itself ("merge step preserves both assessments with attribution") and does not require cross-module handoff verification.
behaviors.test_description: Call merge function with synthetic extractor/risk/compliance outputs containing same clause ID and conflicting labels; assert unified report keeps both labels, source reviewer attribution, and no overwrite.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This is full pipeline behavior spanning multiple reviewer calls plus merge. It sequences multiple agentic outcomes (three live AI reviewers) and checks preservation across orchestration boundaries. Rejected Agentic because text explicitly requires multi-reviewer end-to-end orchestration and aggregation, i.e., more than one agentic step.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline on real contracts through live extractor/risk/compliance reviewers in parallel, then merge; verify final report contains all findings from each reviewer (no dropped clauses/flags/checks) with stable attribution.
